rationale: iter 0 baseline NLPD=1.4036, 0 divergences; trying NCP to improve posterior geometry for group means, which often helps shrinkage estimation in hierarchical models
iter 1 NCP NLPD=1.4035 best; within-group SDs range 0.84-1.21 suggesting heteroscedasticity; adding group-specific sigmas via hierarchical log-normal prior with NCP

notes: NCP + group-specific sigmas + Student-t likelihood (nu estimated)
rationale: iter 2 NLPD=1.4025 best with NCP+heteroscedastic; trying Student-t to handle heavy tails if present; nu estimated from data with gamma(2,0.1) prior

notes: NCP + heteroscedastic + Student-t + tighter priors (tau~N(0,1), tau_sigma~N(0,0.3))
rationale: iter 3 NLPD=1.4018 best; data shows tau~0.86 and within-group SDs 0.84-1.21; tightening priors toward data-informed values to reduce posterior uncertainty

notes: NCP + heteroscedastic + Student-t fixed nu=10 + tighter priors
rationale: iter 4 NLPD=1.4014 best; data is approx normal (Shapiro p=0.24) so fixing nu=10 removes parameter uncertainty while keeping mild robustness; also tightened mu prior to N(0,2)

notes: NCP + heteroscedastic + Student-t nu~gamma(2,0.2) + tighter priors
rationale: iter 5 (fixed nu=10) NLPD=1.4020 worse than iter 4 (1.4014); estimated nu is better; changing nu prior to gamma(2,0.2) (mean=10) to encourage heavier tails vs iter 3/4 gamma(2,0.1) (mean=20)

notes: NCP + heteroscedastic + Normal likelihood + iter-4 priors
rationale: iter 5,6 both non-improving; testing if Student-t genuinely helps vs normal; iter 4 best (1.4014) used Student-t; this isolates the likelihood contribution
